payload.description: _call_claude() in summarize.py uses text=True which cannot handle null bytes in claude output. Fix: use text=False + manual decode with errors=replace. See exophial bug-l2tkm1a.
payload.title: summarize crashes: embedded null byte from subprocess.run text=True
Ingestion does not capture 100% of JSONL session lines in DuckDB
payload.description:
GOAL: every line of every ~/.claude JSONL transcript must become exactly one row in the records table (one row per line, append-only, idempotent, content-faithful). Current ingest_jsonl()/_extract_blobs() in core.py violate this in three ways found during review:

1. MISSING-UUID PK COLLAPSE (silent data loss). core.py:365 uses record.get('uuid', '') and uuid is the records PRIMARY KEY. Every line lacking a uuid maps to the empty-string key; with INSERT OR IGNORE only the FIRST uuid-less record per DB survives and all subsequent uuid-less lines are silently dropped. Fix: synthesize a stable surrogate key for uuid-less lines (e.g. sessionId + line hash or sessionId+offset) instead of '' so each line gets a distinct PK.

2. BLOB OFFLOADING IS UNRECOVERABLE (content not actually in DuckDB). Server always ingests with blob_root set (server.py:147,174). _extract_blobs (core.py:233-297) strips any content block >100KB (BLOB_THRESHOLD_BYTES, blobs.py:24) and replaces it with a '[Large Content: file_id]' marker; the real content goes to the on-disk blob store. read_blob() (blobs.py:67) has ZERO callers, so offloaded content is unreachable through any query/context/summarize path. Net effect: large lines are NOT captured in the queryable store. Fix: either wire read_blob() into the query/context paths to rehydrate, or expose blob content via SQL (e.g. a view/table) so 100% of content is reachable.

3. MALFORMED-LINE + RE-SERIALIZATION CAVEATS. core.py:355 silently skips lines that fail json.loads (acceptable but should be counted/surfaced, not dropped invisibly). Separately, when blob_root is set core.py:361 does line = json.dumps(record) so records.raw is a re-encoded copy, not the original bytes (key order/whitespace/unicode-escaping differ) even for lines with no blob. Verify this is acceptable; if byte-faithfulness is required, store the original line.

Related already-fixed boundary bugs in this same path (is-b3b dropped records under concurrent write/read; is-a2a refresh returning processed:0 for appended lines) show this is the highest-risk area for the 100%-capture invariant. Add a regression test asserting line-count(file) == row-count(records) for a session containing uuid-less lines and >100KB content blocks.

payload.note: Implemented on branch is-a1f-capture-invariant (commit 4599031). 144 tests green incl. live-LLM e2e; pre-commit green. Remaining for close: merge to main, redeploy the running service (uv tool install . + systemctl --user restart ingest-sessions), verify capture_v2 re-ingest against the live DB.

Merged to main (commit 4599031), reinstalled uv tool, restarted systemd service. capture_v2 re-ingest completed in ~13min (checkpoint-bound on a bloated 3.7GB DuckDB file holding only 258MB of live transcripts). Prod-verified via the live MCP query tool on :8741: empty-string PK rows=0, 26940 previously-dropped uuid-less records RECOVERED via surrogate keys, malformed_lines=0, read_blob() SQL round-trip exact (246922 bytes). Service active, NRestarts=0. Pre-migration DB+WAL backed up to ~/.local/share/ingest_sessions/*.bak-pre-isa1f.

payload.description: install.py line 74 hardcodes the Claude Code hook command as: python3 SCRIPT_PATH. Bare system python3 cannot import ingest_sessions because the package lives in the uv-tool ISOLATED venv (~/.local/share/uv/tools/ingest-sessions/...), not system site-packages. So EVERY installed hook (SessionStart session_start + session_retrieve, PreCompact pre_compact, UserPromptSubmit first_prompt_retrieve) fails non-blocking with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ingest_sessions (Traceback on every UserPromptSubmit). Observed on kelce (Ubuntu); system python3.12 present but without the package. FIX: write the command with the installing interpreter, which IS the uv-tool venv python since ingest-sessions-install is a console script in that venv: use sys.executable -m ingest_sessions.hooks.MODULE (module form, no fragile site-packages path), or at minimum sys.executable plus the script path. The python3_available = shutil.which(python3) check at line 171 is the wrong gate: system python3 on PATH does NOT imply it can import the package. Same class as the gateway lint-ratchet env bug (tool resolved off PATH that cannot see the package venv). VERIFIED locally: changing all 4 commands in ~/.claude/settings.json to venv-python -m ingest_sessions.hooks.MODULE resolves the import (session_retrieve injects 1672 chars, first_prompt_retrieve emits hookSpecificOutput, exit 0). Re-running ingest-sessions-install would REINTRODUCE the bug until install.py is fixed. Surfaced while debugging a live UserPromptSubmit hook error in an mtm coordinator session.
payload.title: install.py writes non-portable hook command python3 path; causes ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ingest_sessions

payload.description: Replace the hand-bumped static version (pyproject version = 0.2.0) with a git-derived dynamic version, so the built/installed artifact traces to its build commit (GitOps, matching the gateway version=git_commit model). Static semver already DRIFTED: repo HEAD was several commits past 0.2.0 with no bump (installed 0.2.0 == HEAD only by coincidence). Backend is hatchling, and there are currently ZERO git tags. PRAGMATIC approach chosen by principal (over pure 0.0.0+gSHA): 1) add ONE anchor tag: git tag v0.2.0 on the appropriate commit; 2) pyproject [build-system] requires += hatch-vcs; [project] remove static version, add dynamic = [version]; [tool.hatch.version] source = vcs; 3) versions then become 0.2.0.dev<commits-since-tag>+g<shortsha> — human-readable base AND commit-traceable, PEP 440-valid, never hand-bumped; 4) expose __version__ = importlib.metadata.version(ingest-sessions) in __init__.py (currently absent); 5) reinstall: uv tool install --reinstall <repo>. TARGET DIR: the NESTED project /home/rix/code/git_puller/repos/ingest_sessions/ingest_sessions (holds pyproject.toml + its own .git that hatch-vcs reads). CAVEAT: dynamic versioning needs .git at build time (fine for uv tool install from the repo; hatch-vcs writes _version.py into sdist/wheel for non-git builds). ACCEPTANCE: ingest-sessions --version (or importlib.metadata) reports a git-derived version like 0.2.0.dev3+g<sha>; pyproject no longer carries a static version string; a fresh uv tool install yields a version that traces to the build commit. Pairs with is-dbd (the installer should also write hooks via sys.executable -m, same GitOps/provenance theme).
payload.title: Versioning: switch from static semver to git-derived via hatch-vcs (pragmatic: anchor tag v0.2.0 + dynamic version)
Versioning: replace static semver with pure git-SHA local version (0.0.0+<git-sha>) via hatchling

payload.description: Replace the hand-bumped static version (pyproject version = 0.2.0) with a PURE git-commit version: 0.0.0+<short-git-sha>. NO anchor tag, NO .dev<distance>, NO semver base — the version IS the build commit (GitOps; matches the gateway version=git_commit model). The static semver already drifted (repo HEAD was past 0.2.0 with no bump). IMPLEMENTATION (hatchling-native, no setuptools-scm tag machinery): a custom hatchling version source — hatch_build.py implementing a VersionSourceInterface (or [tool.hatch.version] source pointing at a small function) that returns '0.0.0+g' + (git rev-parse --short HEAD). pyproject: [project] add dynamic = [version], remove the static version; keep build-backend hatchling. Expose __version__ = importlib.metadata.version(ingest-sessions) in __init__.py. Reinstall: uv tool install --reinstall <repo>. WHY drop the tag/.dev<N>+gSHA scheme: that exists only so PyPI packages get monotonic, pip-upgrade-orderable versions — irrelevant for a uv-tool-from-git install, so it's pure overhead. PEP 440 note: 0.0.0+<sha> is valid (the +segment is a local version); it is NOT PyPI-uploadable, which is fine since this is installed from the repo, not published. TARGET DIR: the NESTED project /home/rix/code/git_puller/repos/ingest_sessions/ingest_sessions (pyproject + its .git). CAVEAT: needs .git at build (fine for uv tool install from repo; the version source can fall back to 0.0.0+unknown if .git absent). ACCEPTANCE: ingest-sessions --version (or importlib.metadata) reports 0.0.0+g<sha> matching the build commit; pyproject carries no static version and no tags are involved. Pairs with is-dbd (installer should write hooks via sys.executable -m — same provenance theme).

payload.body: REFINEMENT (principal): version ordering only matters at PyPI publish (an index resolves 'latest'); a uv-tool-from-git install pins the exact ref, so the version is provenance-only. So: SHA-local by default, a real minor number ONLY when publishing. Don't hardcode 0.0.0 permanently — make the custom version source: if HEAD is on an exact tag (git describe --exact-match --tags) emit that tag (e.g. 0.2.0, the PyPI release version); ELSE emit 0.0.0+g<short-sha>. No .dev<N> distance, no semver guessing. Net: every dev/tool build is 0.0.0+g<sha>; publishing to PyPI is just 'git tag 0.2.0' on the release commit (the local +sha is dropped, PyPI gets a clean 0.2.0). This is the whole reconciliation of 'SHA now' + 'minor number for pypi later' in one ~5-line source.

CORRECTION: drop the 'g' prefix — use 0.0.0+<short-sha> (e.g. 0.0.0+1409a31), NOT 0.0.0+g1409a31. The 'g' is just git-describe convention (g=git) with zero functional value; PEP 440 accepts the bare hex local segment fine. Final scheme: tagged commit -> the tag (PyPI minor); else 0.0.0+<short-sha>.

FIXED: _hook_command now returns '<sys.executable> -m ingest_sessions.hooks.<module>' (module form under the installing interpreter = the uv-tool venv python that can import the package), replacing the non-portable 'python3 <path>'. Removed the wrong python3_available = shutil.which('python3') gate (system python3 on PATH != can import the uv-isolated package); check_hooks now reports 'interpreter': sys.executable, and the misleading 'python3 not found' warning is gone. Added regression test test_hook_command_uses_sys_executable_module_form asserting the module form and rejecting the bare 'python3 <path>.py' form. Full suite green (286 passed); pre-commit green. Tokens: ~55k (~$0.4 est).

payload.description:
When a session JSONL file is ingested while the Claude session is still active, lines written after the initial ingest are never picked up. Calling refresh with the file path returns processed: 0 — it appears to treat the file as already fully ingested.

Example: session f53bf7a0-36f8-493f-84c5-d7251f8c3c02 has 1347 lines in the JSONL file but only 910 records in the database. The final 4 assistant messages (timestamps 17:05-17:08) were written to the file after the initial ingest and are missing from the database. refresh does not detect or process these new lines.

Expected: refresh should detect appended lines (e.g. by comparing line count or file size against what was previously ingested) and ingest only the new records.
payload.title: refresh does not ingest lines appended after initial ingestion
Assistant messages with plain text content silently dropped during ingestion
payload.description:
Assistant messages where the content field is a plain string (not a list of content blocks) are silently dropped during ingestion.

Evidence: Session d473cc02-1874-43f5-81e0-761a9691af0c has 1884 records in the database, but an assistant message at timestamp 2026-03-16T07:30:31 is missing. The message exists in the JSONL file (confirmed via grep) with plain text content: "No. Kind can't bind the same ports twice..."

The preceding user message ("do we ever have a bug where there are two kind clusters running at once?") IS in the database. The following user message is also present. Only the assistant response between them is missing.

The assistant message content is a plain string, not a [{"type":"text","text":"..."}] array. Other assistant messages that use the array format are ingested correctly. The parser likely expects list-of-blocks format and skips plain string messages.

Reproduction:
1. Query: SELECT timestamp, type FROM records WHERE session_id = 'd473cc02-...' AND timestamp BETWEEN '2026-03-16 07:30:00' AND '2026-03-16 07:32:00'
2. Note the gap: user at 07:30:31, then user at 07:31:48 — no assistant record between them
3. grep "bind the same ports" on the raw JSONL file confirms the message exists

payload.title: Session summaries missing raw JSONL content
payload.description: Session 9a4e3f7e-eff9-41dd-bb1d-19f5132576c5 (and likely 749726c1-f5c7-4834-b073-98129b753154) appear to have lost their raw message content. The sessions table contains only chain summaries in the first_prompt field, not the original conversation. The summaries table is empty for these sessions. The raw JSONL files on disk should be preserved — ingest-sessions must never delete source JSONL files, only read from them.

## Gap

ingest-sessions has no first-class way to retrieve a FULL session by session_id. Today only:

- `context(session_id)` returns the LCM summary-DAG recovery context (a SUMMARY, not the full session).
- `query(sql)` is manual SQL over `records`; you can `SELECT raw FROM records WHERE session_id=? ORDER BY timestamp`, but large content blocks are offloaded to a blob store (`[Large Content: file_...]`, rehydrated via `read_blob(file_id)`), so full reconstruction is clunky and easy to get wrong.

There is no get-full-session primitive: context=summary, query=SQL-with-blob-offload.

## Desired

A tool/endpoint, e.g. `get_session(session_id, {include_blobs=true, format})`, that returns the full transcript reassembled in chronological order with all `[Large Content: file_...]` markers rehydrated via read_blob, optionally paginated/streamed for large sessions, and a stable citation form (so a labbook `session_id` resolves to the actual content).

## Use case

Durable cross-repo citation. labbook/ADR entries now reference sessions by id (e.g. mtm `docs/labbook/2026-06-22-verification-is-ast-decidable-or-observational-not-semantic.md`) as the canonical, durable reference of record — the mtm labbook now treats ingest-sessions as the session reference of record, not the filesystem. An amnesiac agent must be able to retrieve the cited session's full content on demand from just the id.

## Acceptance

Given a known `session_id`, the tool returns the complete ordered transcript with blobs rehydrated; round-trips a session whose content exceeds the inline blob threshold.
payload.title: Add first-class full-session retrieval by session_id (ordered records + blob rehydration)

payload.description:
## Why
Agents are anterograde-amnesiac: durable RECORD is solved (sessions are stored here; curated findings live in consuming repos as labbook/ADR), but RECALL is not — nothing surfaces relevant prior reasoning into a fresh session BY CONSTRUCTION. ingest-sessions should become the on-device retrieval BRAIN; a thin Claude Code UserPromptSubmit/SessionStart hook injects whatever it returns (hook stdout / hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext is injected as context — Claude Code hooks docs https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks).

## Corpus decision: ALL sessions, not just curated derivatives
Curation is the unreliable step (the amnesiac can't be trusted to distill), so restricting retrieval to curated docs caps recall at the distilled fraction. The raw sessions are PRIMARY SOURCE, not lossy auto-summaries — retrieve over them. BUT raw sessions contain dead ends, errors, and later-REVERSED conclusions, so retrieval MUST be supersession/confidence-aware or it surfaces overturned reasoning as settled (is-X.2).

## Architecture (the seam — keep this repo project-AGNOSTIC)
- BRAIN here: corpus + embedding/BM25 index + cross-encoder rerank + supersession/confidence graph + retrieval API. On-device (latency: the per-prompt hook is synchronous, sub-second budget) and reused cross-repo via MCP.
- Thin adapters OUTSIDE (per consuming repo, e.g. mtm): (a) artifact->supersession-link adapter mapping that repo's git reverts / issue-tracker closes / ADR amendments into the GENERAL supersession-link form this repo ingests; (b) the harness hook that calls the retrieval API and formats injection. Do NOT hardcode any consumer's tracker/ADR semantics here.

## Children
- is-X.1 retrieve_relevant API (two-stage retrieve -> cross-encoder rerank, on-device).
- is-X.2 supersession + confidence + trust-tier ranking (the hard part / differentiator).
- Existing is-6f5 (get_full_session by id) is the on-demand FETCH primitive of this same surface — reframe as member of the retrieval API family.

## Prior art (this is a known pattern; reuse, don't reinvent the retrieval stack)
- ClawMem (yoloshii) — on-device hooks+MCP, hybrid RAG: BM25 + vector + RRF + query expansion + cross-encoder rerank + composite scoring (recency decay, confidence, content-type half-lives): https://github.com/yoloshii/ClawMem
- mann1x/claude-hooks — Qdrant + memory KG, HyDE, attention decay, 'deterministic memory recall': https://github.com/mann1x/claude-hooks
- claude-mnemonic (lukaszraczylo): https://github.com/lukaszraczylo/claude-mnemonic ; claude-mem (auto-capture + inject) https://docs.claude-mem.ai/hooks-architecture
- 'Storage Is Solved, Injection Isn't' (the gap is relevance/injection): https://alexandrekhoury.com/writing/superbrain-session-memory-claude-code
- Hindsight is 20/20 — agent memory retain/recall/reflect (arXiv): https://arxiv.org/pdf/2512.12818
THE GAP none of them close: explicit SUPERSESSION linking + primary-source-vs-auto-summary TRUST-TIERING. They retrieve auto-extracted summaries flatly; we rank raw primary source with supersession awareness. That is the novel work (is-X.2).
payload.title: Supersession-aware relevance-retrieval surface (hook-time context injection over all sessions)

payload.description:
First-class retrieval API alongside context()/query(). Stage 1: BM25 + vector candidate retrieval (sqlite-vec + local embeddings, e.g. all-MiniLM-L6-v2) over records. Stage 2: cross-encoder rerank (e.g. cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2) jointly scoring (query, candidate) -> relevance. PRETRAINED zero-shot — no project labels at cold start. On-device + sub-second (the per-prompt UserPromptSubmit hook is synchronous; remote calls blow the budget). Query = prompt + recent transcript/context. Returns ranked records + pointers + get_full_session ids (is-6f5) for on-demand deepening. Two stages are required over 9000+ sessions (don't rerank-all). Ranking policy is is-565's other child (supersession/confidence/trust-tier); this pebble is the retrieve+rerank pipeline. Parent: is-565.

Sources: ClawMem hybrid pipeline https://github.com/yoloshii/ClawMem ; cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 ; sqlite-vec + all-MiniLM-L6-v2 ; Claude Code hooks (injection target) https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks

Acceptance: given (query, context) returns top-k ranked records sub-second on-device; integrates get_full_session (is-6f5); pretrained reranker works with zero project training.
payload.title: retrieve_relevant(query, ctx): two-stage on-device retrieval -> cross-encoder rerank

payload.description:
Rank candidates by relevance x recency-decay x confidence x NOT-superseded, so retrieval never surfaces an overturned or mid-flight-wrong statement as settled (raw sessions are full of dead ends, errors, reversals). Parent: is-565.

1. SUPERSESSION (novel; nobody does this): ingest 'record A supersedes record B' links in a GENERAL form, fed by per-consumer adapters that translate git reverts / issue-tracker closes / ADR amendments (those semantics stay OUT of this repo). Down-weight/flag superseded records; derive simple cases internally where observable.
2. CONFIDENCE + RECENCY decay (prior art): ClawMem composite scoring (recency decay, confidence, content-type half-lives) https://github.com/yoloshii/ClawMem ; mann1x/claude-hooks attention decay https://github.com/mann1x/claude-hooks .
3. TRUST-TIER: weight raw PRIMARY-SOURCE records above the LCM auto-summary tier (sprigs/bindles); an auto-summary must never outrank a contradicting primary-source record. This repo already has both tiers — this is ranking over them.

GAP these tools leave: flat retrieval of auto-extracted summaries with decay but NO explicit supersession-linking and NO primary-vs-summary trust-tier. That is this pebble.

Acceptance: for a query where a plan was chosen then later removed (build-then-revert arc), the superseding conclusion outranks/flags the reversed one; the auto-summary tier never outranks a contradicting primary-source record.
payload.title: Supersession + confidence + trust-tier ranking (the hard part / differentiator)

payload.body: Verified already fixed. Root cause + fix landed in commit 9b0f308 (is-a2a: refresh returning processed:0 for files with appended lines): ingest_jsonl() now returns (count, bytes_read) via f.tell(); record_file() records the byte offset actually consumed (not path.stat().st_size), and the mid-line seek skip is conditioned on the preceding byte being a newline. Regression coverage in tests/test_regression_2026_03.py (test_append_then_refresh_finds_new_records, test_record_file_uses_explicit_size_not_stat, test_ingest_jsonl_returns_byte_offset, test_clean_boundary_offset_does_not_skip_first_line) — full suite 144 passed. Tracker was simply never closed.

Root cause fixed by is-a1f (commit 4599031: 'Enforce 100% JSONL line capture in DuckDB'). The reported symptom — sessions losing raw message content — was the uuid-less-record collapse: every record without a uuid mapped onto the empty-string PK, so INSERT OR IGNORE silently dropped all but the first. is-a1f gives uuid-less records deterministic surrogate keys (sha256 of line bytes), captures unparseable lines in malformed_lines, and migrate_capture_v2 clears file_mtimes so the next pass re-ingests every file from offset 0, recovering previously-dropped lines. Enforced going forward by tests/test_capture_invariant.py. The two specific sessions named here (9a4e3f7e-…, 749726c1-…) cannot be re-verified post-hoc: their source JSONL files no longer exist under ~/.claude/projects, and the runtime DB now holds 2 records each (a SessionStart hook + one user prompt) — consistent with short sessions, with no source to re-ingest. Closing: root cause fixed + invariant-tested; specific historical data is unrecoverable (source files gone), not a live code defect.

Implemented on branch is-565.1-get-session (commit 59796a4). New module src/ingest_sessions/retrieval.py with get_full_session(db, session_id, *, include_blobs, blob_root) — reassembles the transcript chronologically (timestamp NULLS LAST, uuid tiebreak) and rehydrates every [Large Content: file_...] marker via one recursive walk (covers string/text-block/tool_result/nested shapes); missing blobs preserved+reported, never blanked. Plus format_session_text(). Wired as MCP tool get_session (session_id, include_blobs, format) and POST /api/session, both through the single DB thread. Acceptance met: server e2e (stdio) round-trips a >100KB session ingest->get_session with isolated blob store; blob marker rehydrated to original content. Full suite 153 passed, ruff + mypy --strict clean, pre-commit green. Cost: ~95k tokens (coordinator, no worker dispatched). Status: verified on branch, pending merge to main.
Merged to main (merge commit 50d9b8b, --no-ff). get_session live in src; runtime MCP server picks it up on next restart. Done: integrated + e2e-verified + merged.

Decisions (2026-06-22): DuckDB-native vss(HNSW)+fts(BM25), NOT sqlite-vec; ONNX via fastembed (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 + ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2); backfill whole corpus (~857k) via resumable mechanism (full run is operational, not a unit test); dispatched to pipelined TDD workers. Slices: 2a embeddings+vss; 2b fts+RRF->retrieve_candidates; 2c rerank->retrieve_relevant+MCP. Design: docs/plans/2026-06-22-retrieval-brain-is-565.md

GROUNDWORK landed (merged to main, merge of b7dc577). The project-agnostic supersession LINK STORE is in place: supersessions table (superseding_id/superseded_id/source/created_at + index) in core.create_tables(); functional-core API in src/ingest_sessions/supersession.py (add_supersession idempotent + self-link ValueError, add_supersessions batch, is_superseded, get_superseded_ids, get_supersessions_for); ingest seam = add_supersession MCP tool + POST /api/supersessions (single or batch). No provenance parsing here (adapters live outside repo), and NO ranking composition yet. Verified independently: full suite 172 passed, ruff + mypy + pre-commit green. REMAINING for this pebble: the ranking composition (relevance × recency-decay × confidence × not-superseded × trust-tier) that consumes this table — blocked on the is-565.2 retrieve+rerank pipeline (2a/2b/2c). Worker cost: ~95k tokens (~$1.0 est).

Slice 2a MERGED to main (merge cc53835): the vector arm — embeddings.py (fastembed all-MiniLM-L6-v2 384-dim, lazy singleton, resumable backfill_embeddings, search_vector) + record_embeddings sidecar table + DuckDB vss HNSW cosine index in core.load_vss/create_tables. Integrated suite 180 passed (verified against the supersession merge; core.py create_tables conflict resolved keeping both tables). Next: 2b (fts BM25 + RRF -> retrieve_candidates) in flight; then 2c (cross-encoder rerank -> retrieve_relevant + MCP tool).

COMPLETE — all three slices merged: 2a vector (embeddings+vss HNSW), 2b lexical+RRF (retrieve_candidates), 2c cross-encoder rerank (retrieve_relevant, wired MCP tool + POST /api/retrieve). Two-stage on-device per acceptance: stage-1 RRF-fuses vector(all-MiniLM-L6-v2)+BM25 -> bounded candidates; stage-2 reranks (Xenova/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2); zero project training; returns top-k with uuid/session_id as the get_full_session deepening point. Integrated suite 198 passed; gates green. Operational note: backfill_embeddings + rebuild_fts_index must be run once over the live corpus (graceful [] until then; not auto-run at startup). Worker cost ~250k tokens across 2a/2b/2c.

COMPLETE — ranking composition merged to main (83bfb35). New ranking.py: final = relevance(sigmoid of cross-encoder logit) × recency_decay(30d half-life) × confidence(type heuristic) × not_superseded(0.05 penalty, flagged not deleted) × trust_tier(0.5 summary penalty); pure compose_score + rank_candidates (injected now_ms). Wired into retrieve_relevant (reads supersession.get_superseded_ids at boundary). HEADLINE ACCEPTANCE PROVEN e2e with real models: build-then-revert arc ranks the superseding conclusion above the reverted plan and flags it superseded, even when the plan's raw rerank score is higher. SEAM: trust-tier floor is implemented + unit-tested (synthetic mixed-tier candidates) but NOT yet e2e — the live pipeline only indexes the primary records tier; indexing the summaries auto-tier as a retrieval candidate source is a follow-up (filed separately). Supersession + recency + confidence ARE e2e. Suite 211 passed; gates green. Worker cost ~75k tokens.

payload.description:
Follow-up from is-565.3. The ranking trust-tier floor (an auto-summary must never outrank a contradicting primary-source record) is implemented + unit-tested in ranking.py but NOT exercised end-to-end: retrieve_candidates only retrieves from the primary records tier (record_embeddings + record_fts). The summaries table (LCM sprigs/bindles) is not a retrieval candidate source, so retrieve_relevant never returns a summary-tier candidate for the trust-tier penalty to act on.

Scope: extend the stage-1 retrieval (is-565.2 territory) so summaries are also embedded (a summaries embedding sidecar + vss) and BM25-indexed, surfaced as candidates tagged tier='summary', and fused alongside primary records. Then the is-565.3 trust-tier becomes e2e: a contradicting primary record outranks a summary of equal raw relevance. Add an e2e test for that ordering against the live pipeline. Parent: is-565.
payload.title: Index the summaries auto-tier as a retrieval candidate source (make trust-tier e2e)

payload.body:
BRAIN COMPLETE (this repo's responsibility). Children landed on main this session: is-565.1 get_session (FETCH primitive, full transcript + blob rehydration), is-565.2 retrieve_relevant (two-stage on-device: vector all-MiniLM-L6-v2 + BM25 fused via RRF -> cross-encoder ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 rerank), is-565.3 ranking composition (supersession down-weight + recency + confidence + trust-tier, supersession e2e-proven via build-then-revert). All DuckDB-native (vss + fts), ONNX via fastembed, project-agnostic. Suite 211 passed; all gates green; each slice TDD'd, independently verified, --no-ff merged.

REMAINING (NOT this repo's code, per the epic's seam):
1. Operational: run the full-corpus backfill once (backfill_embeddings + rebuild_fts_index over ~857k records) so retrieve_relevant returns non-empty; requires the MCP server running the new code. Until then it degrades gracefully to [].
2. Consumer-side (OUTSIDE this repo): the thin Claude Code UserPromptSubmit/SessionStart hook that calls /api/retrieve and formats injection, + the artifact->supersession-link adapter feeding add_supersession (git reverts / tracker closes / ADR amendments).
3. is-565.4 (filed): index the summaries auto-tier so the trust-tier floor becomes e2e.

payload.description:
After the one-time full backfill (99.97%, 869968/870236), the in-server backfill uses keyset-from-max(uuid). Since session uuids are random (uuid4), records ingested AFTER the cursor passed their uuid range are never picked up by a keyset-from-max re-run -> coverage silently decays as new sessions arrive (the ~268 current stragglers are this effect).

Fix for ONGOING 100% coverage (two complementary options):
1. Embed at INGESTION time: in server _ingest_file_full / _ingest_all, after inserting records, embed the new batch (off the DB thread) so new sessions are covered by construction. This is the durable answer.
2. Make the periodic backfill use the anti-join (LEFT JOIN record_embeddings WHERE e.uuid IS NULL) rather than keyset-from-max, so a re-run catches ANY unembedded record regardless of uuid (slower full scan, acceptable for a periodic sweep). Keep keyset only for the initial bulk run.

Also wire a periodic FTS rebuild (record_fts is a snapshot; new records aren't searchable lexically until rebuilt). Parent: is-565.
payload.title: Ongoing embedding coverage: embed at ingestion + anti-join backfill (keyset misses post-cursor low-uuid inserts)

payload.body:
CORE DELIVERED + PROD-VERIFIED (2026-06-23). The on-device retrieval brain is live on markto... (the ingest-sessions systemd service) and prod-verified end-to-end:
- is-565.1 get_session, is-565.2 retrieve_relevant (vector all-MiniLM-L6-v2 + BM25 fts fused via RRF -> ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 cross-encoder rerank -> recency/confidence/supersession/trust-tier ranking), is-565.3 supersession link store — all merged to main, pushed (AFDudley/ingest_sessions), deployed (uv tool reinstall + systemd restart), running in the live service.
- Full-corpus backfill ran IN-SERVER with zero downtime (POST /api/backfill): 869,968/870,236 records embedded (99.97%) + 870,233 FTS rows. retrieve_relevant prod-verified returning relevant ranked hits (rerank 6+, final ~0.99) over the full corpus.
Suite 215 green; all slices TDD'd + independently verified + --no-ff merged.

REMAINING (open children, not blocking core):
- is-565.4: index the summaries auto-tier so the trust-tier floor is exercised e2e (currently primary-records tier only).
- is-590: ongoing coverage — embed at ingestion + anti-join periodic backfill (keyset-from-max misses post-cursor low-uuid inserts; ~268 stragglers).
- Consumer-side (OUTSIDE repo, per the epic seam): the Claude Code UserPromptSubmit/SessionStart hook calling /api/retrieve + the artifact->supersession-link adapter feeding /api/supersessions.

payload.description: Populate real supersession links and make ranking honor session-grained supersession (the is-565.3 ranking was record-uuid-only; real sources are session/doc grained). Delivered: ranking.rank_candidates + retrieve_relevant honor superseded_session_ids (a record is down-weighted if its session_id is superseded; grain resolved by lookup against records.session_id, no schema change); scripts/supersession_adapter.py (project-agnostic) scans docs/decisions + docs/labbook for Superseded-by/Supersedes relations resolving to each artifact's cited Session: <uuid> -> session-level links, plus git-revert commits (recorded but INERT for ranking — no sha->session mapping, labelled honestly). Parent: is-565.
payload.title: Supersession adapter + session-level supersession ranking

payload.description:
DISCOVERED in prod 2026-06-23 at ~873k records. core.rebuild_fts_index does, ON THE SINGLE DB THREAD: SELECT all ~873k raw, DELETE record_fts, recompute json.loads(raw)+record_text for every row (Python, one thread), executemany INSERT, then PRAGMA create_fts_index. This blocks ALL queries (every _db_execute) for ~45 MINUTES. The periodic sync (is-565.5) calls it whenever embedded>0, so any trickle of new sessions re-triggers a 45-min service-wide stall every cycle. MITIGATION IN PLACE: systemd drop-in sets INGEST_SESSIONS_SYNC_INTERVAL=86400 (~/.config/systemd/user/ingest-sessions.service.d/override.conf) to effectively disable the periodic sweep; remove it once fixed.

Also NON-ATOMIC: a restart during the rebuild left record_fts partial (90,053 of 873,713 rows) while the fts_main_record_fts index snapshot is from the prior full build -> inconsistent lexical state (vector arm unaffected; retrieve_relevant still works, lexical recall degraded).

FIX:
1. Build the new (uuid,text) projection OFF the DB thread (json.loads+record_text in asyncio.to_thread, chunked); do only the DB writes + create_fts_index on the DB thread, ideally as a single atomic transaction (build shadow table -> swap) so an interrupt rolls back cleanly.
2. Decouple FTS-rebuild cadence from every embed: throttle (e.g. at most every N hours, or only when record count grew by >X%), since DuckDB FTS is snapshot-only (no incremental).
3. After the fix, run ONE clean full rebuild to repair the current partial record_fts.
Parent: is-565 (regression from is-565.5 sync path).
payload.title: FTS rebuild blocks the DB thread ~45min at corpus scale + non-atomic (partial on interrupt)

payload.body: DELIVERED + PROD-VERIFIED. Merged (34baf24), deployed; live server now has summary_embeddings populated 4534/4534. retrieve_relevant returns mixed-tier candidates with correct tier tags; trust-tier floor proven e2e (real models, test_trust_tier_floor_fires_end_to_end). Cost ~95k tokens.

FIXED + DEPLOYED + REPAIRED + MEASURED. Merged (00430b0/e5e159a), deployed, mitigation drop-in removed (FTS throttle now protects). Repair rebuild ran on the live 874k-record corpus: parse off-thread (DB served, 4.3s during parse), residual INSERT+create_fts_index block measured at ~3.3 min (22:25:55->22:29:03) — down from ~45 min (~13x), throttled to <=1/hr. record_fts repaired 90,053 -> 874,046 (== records). Lexical+vector both full. Cost ~85k tokens.

DELIVERED + DEPLOYED. Periodic anti-join sync keeps record_embeddings + summary_embeddings in sync by construction (catches the keyset gap); FTS rebuild now off-thread/atomic/throttled (is-e10). Live: 874,046/874,068 records embedded, periodic sync re-enabled at 300s with FTS throttle 3600s. Cost ~115k tokens.

Code DELIVERED + deployed (session-level supersession ranking live; adapter scripts/supersession_adapter.py merged). BUT populating real links is BLOCKED on source data: dry-run against the mtm checkout finds 0 session-level links and 0 mappable git-reverts. mtm's ADRs DO declare supersession (e.g. 2026-06-02-main-is-canon supersedes 2026-04-16-afd-rewrite-is-canon; mtm-agent-retired supersedes refs) via **Supersedes:** headers, but they reference other ADRs BY PATH and carry NO 'Session: <uuid>' citation line — so neither end resolves to a corpus session_id and no session-level link can be emitted. To actually update the supersessions, the consuming repo (mtm) must adopt a session-of-record citation convention in its ADR/labbook entries (a 'Session: <uuid>' line), then run the adapter. Until then supersessions stays empty and the (working, tested) supersession ranking has no data. Not a code defect — a missing upstream convention. Keeping OPEN, blocked on that convention.

STATUS 2026-06-23: brain fully built, deployed, prod-verified over the live 874k-record corpus. Closed: is-565.1 get_session, is-565.2 retrieve_relevant, is-565.3 supersession schema, is-565.4 summaries tier, is-565.5 ongoing sync, is-e10 FTS off-thread/atomic/throttled fix. retrieve_relevant returns relevant, correctly-tiered, supersession-aware ranked results live; record_embeddings 874,046/874,068 + summary_embeddings 4,534 + record_fts 874,046 all in sync.

REMAINING:
1. SessionStart injection HOOK (consumer-side, ~/.claude) — NOT built. THE payoff: nothing surfaces retrieval into sessions yet. Latency-scoped: per-prompt UserPromptSubmit infeasible (1.5-4s warm), use SessionStart (~2.5s once/session).
2. is-3cd: populate supersessions — adapter ready but BLOCKED on mtm adopting a 'Session: <uuid>' ADR/labbook citation convention (currently 0 mappable links).
3. Minor/optional: create_fts_index residual ~3.5min DB-thread block (throttled <=1/hr) — acceptable; future optimization only.

payload.description: The injection half of the epic — agents recall prior reasoning by construction. hooks/session_retrieve.py seeds a query from the repo's git focus (branch + recent commit subjects), calls POST /api/retrieve, injects top-5 as additionalContext with get_session citations + [superseded] flags; fails open. Server warms embed+rerank models at HTTP startup so the hook is reliably warm. Registered in ~/.claude/settings.json alongside session_start.py.
payload.title: SessionStart hook: inject relevant prior reasoning (retrieve_relevant)

payload.body: DELIVERED + DEPLOYED + REGISTERED + PROD-VERIFIED. Merged (0945d6d/dd485df), pushed, tool redeployed, service restarted (models warmed 1s post-startup), hook registered in ~/.claude/settings.json. E2E against the live server: built query from this repo's git focus, returned top-5 relevant prior sessions with citations/scores/superseded flags; steady-state latency ~2.4s (first-after-restart ~9.6s incl. HNSW-index load, within the 15s budget); valid JSON additionalContext (1672 chars). Suite 264 passed. Cost: coordinator-built, ~110k tokens this round.

CORE SURFACE DELIVERED + LIVE (2026-06-24). The epic's headline — hook-time context injection over all sessions — is ACHIEVED and prod-verified: the SessionStart hook (is-58b) injects retrieve_relevant output (vector+BM25 -> cross-encoder -> recency/confidence/supersession/trust-tier ranking) into fresh sessions, deployed + registered, latency ~2.4s warm. Full brain (is-565.1 get_session, is-565.2 retrieve_relevant, is-565.3 supersession schema, is-565.4 summaries tier, is-565.5 ongoing sync, is-565.7/is-58b injection hook, is-e10 FTS fix) is merged, pushed, deployed, running over the live 874k-record corpus. ONLY REMAINING: is-565.6 (populate real supersession links) — BLOCKED on the consuming repo adopting a 'Session: <uuid>' ADR/labbook citation convention; the ranking + adapter are ready and will light up once that data exists.

payload.description:
Symptom (reported 2026-06-24): the is-565.7 session_retrieve.py SessionStart hook injects the 'Relevant prior reasoning' block when a session starts via 'claude --resume' (source=resume) but NOT on a clean 'claude' (source=startup).

Evidence gathered:
- The hook outputs valid hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext JSON and exits 0 for ALL sources (startup/resume/clear) — verified by running the installed hook with each source.
- Server journal shows every POST /api/retrieve returns 200 OK with NO timeouts/errors — so the hook reaches the server and gets results; the cold-start-timeout hypothesis is RULED OUT.
- Claude Code hooks docs (per claude-code-guide review) state SessionStart additionalContext should inject 'before the first prompt' for ALL sources with no documented startup/resume difference — so this is a contract violation (likely a Claude Code harness bug, or the hook is simply not INVOKED on fresh startup).

Open diagnostic: we cannot yet tell 'hook not invoked on startup' from 'invoked but additionalContext dropped' because the hook logs nothing on invocation.

FIX (robust, in our control — do not depend on the flaky SessionStart-startup path):
1. Add stderr invocation logging to session_retrieve.py (log source + whether it produced injection) so startup-vs-resume invocation is observable in the journal next time.
2. Add a guaranteed once-per-session injection via a UserPromptSubmit hook gated by a per-session marker (e.g. a file keyed by session_id under a cache dir): on the FIRST user prompt of a session, if no marker, inject the same retrieve_relevant context and write the marker; subsequent prompts skip. Adds ~2.4s to only the first prompt; guarantees the feature works even when SessionStart-startup drops additionalContext. Keep the SessionStart hook (free + works on resume/clear).
3. Register the new hook in install.py; tests + e2e.
4. Separately: report the SessionStart-startup additionalContext gap to Claude Code via /feedback (manual).
payload.title: SessionStart retrieve hook injects on resume/clear but NOT on fresh 'claude' startup

payload.body: FIXED + DEPLOYED + REGISTERED. Root cause: Claude Code drops SessionStart additionalContext on source=startup (server returns 200 OK every call; hook outputs valid JSON for all sources — confirmed harness-side, not ours). Fix (e7413b1/dd83294, merged + pushed): shared hooks/_retrieve_common.py + a UserPromptSubmit hook (first_prompt_retrieve.py) that injects once per session on the first prompt, gated by a per-session marker where a source=startup SessionStart marker is recorded but NOT trusted -> injection is guaranteed at-least-once even when startup is dropped, exactly-once normally. Plus stderr invocation logging on session_retrieve for future diagnosis. Live e2e: startup marker doesn't suppress -> first prompt injects (1672 chars) -> second prompt skips. Deployed (tool reinstalled, service restarted, hook registered in settings.json). Suite 282 passed. Remaining manual step: report the SessionStart-startup additionalContext gap to Claude Code via /feedback. Cost ~115k tokens.

payload.description:
Found via mtm-259. SESSION_CITATION_RE / SUPERSEDED_BY_RE / SUPERSEDES_RE in scripts/supersession_adapter.py match a PLAIN label ('Session: <uuid>', 'Superseded by: <ref>') but NOT the markdown-bold form ('**Session:** <uuid>') — the '**' between the colon and the value breaks the pattern, so a bold citation silently yields ZERO links. Verified: SESSION_CITATION_RE.search('Session: <uuid>')=True, search('**Session:** <uuid>')=False.

mtm's existing ADR headers are all bold (**Status:**, **Supersedes:**), so bold is the NATURAL style; the mtm-259 convention works around this by MANDATING plain lines (documented in the ADR + templates), which is correct-by-construction and prevents the failure. So this is OPTIONAL robustness, NOT load-bearing: make the three regexes tolerate optional leading/trailing '**' or '__' emphasis around the label+colon, so a bold citation parses instead of silently failing. LOW priority — the plain convention already prevents the footgun; this just removes the sharp edge for anyone who writes bold by habit. Parent: is-565.
payload.title: supersession_adapter regexes don't parse markdown-bold labels (**Session:** silently emits 0)

payload.body: DONE end-to-end. Session-level supersession ranking + the project-agnostic adapter are built, merged, deployed. The mtm session-of-record citation convention (mtm-259) is merged to mtm main; afd-rewrite/main-is-canon retrofitted with their real producing sessions (b3b9cc53, fe1cadfd — found by timestamp+content, no fabrication). Adapter run against live mtm -> 1 real session-level link inserted (fe1cadfd supersedes b3b9cc53). Verified e2e: retrieve_relevant down-weights the superseded session so it cannot surface even for exact-content queries while non-superseded peers on the same topic rank above it. Also fixed a found bug (1409a31): the adapter parsed code-fence/template examples as data -> false links; now strips code + skips TEMPLATE.md (regression-tested, 285 green). Remaining: is-565.8 (P4, optional) adapter bold-label tolerance.
